I'm concerned about circularity of the inputs and outputs of AI models in social science.  AIs trained on data at T1 will be used to tell us things about society at T2 (via a black box).  This means AIs interpretation of T2 will necessarily be filtered through their understanding of T1.  In other words, AI takes classic concerns about researcher's understandings being filtered through a perspective (which qualitative researchers have long worried about) and amplifies them, since we will not longer be able to observe these biases/effects of these perspectives as directly.

ai_risk_open: Intellectual property issues

One of the main reasons we do research is that we can't reliably predict the results of human-subjects studies (especially given the variability across individuals). Why would AI be able to predict these unknown results? AI assumes that the future resembles the past, which is not always true. AI also favors the dominant/majority in the data, which means that inaccuracies, even if proportionally tiny, can disproportionally distort the results for minorities/underrepresend groups/edge and corner cases, etc. So in some sense, the premise of this study (that AI can "accurately" predict results of human studies) seems to be flawed. (And "acccurate" according to what definition?)

But let's assume such "accurate" AI can and does exist. If so, what's the purpose/point of conducting any human studies at all? Just use the "accurate" AI to predict the results. Done!

AI reproduces human speech that it learns from internet data often obtained without the consent of the people generating this data. To think that this means it can reproduce the thoughts and actions of humans is not only unethical (since it is based on a model that uses unconsented data) but also tautological (“humans think/do this because humans think/do this (as evidenced from the possibly illegally scraped internet data we used to train our model)”). At that point, just look at internet data yourself - it will save you the carbon footprint and prevent you from violating your IRB guidelines.
